methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $1.1B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.6B - $250.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $1.6B

data.narrative:
Modal is a Series E-stage AI & Software company founded by Erik Bernhardsson and Akshat Bubna, currently valued at $1.1B post-money after raising $111M. The relevant comp universe consists of 9 private peers in the same sector and stage, alongside 1 public comparable (Palantir).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical for Series E advancement, implies $1.5B from the $1.1B last post-money valuation. Peer-median methodology examined five same-sector, same-stage companies: xAI ($250.0B), Crusoe Energy ($10.0B), SandboxAQ ($5.6B), Ayar Labs ($3.8B), and WEKA ($1.6B). The median across this set is $5.6B, with a range spanning $1.6B to $250.0B. The wide dispersion reflects significant variance in AI sector valuations at this stage, driven by differing business models and capital intensity.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $1.3B (low) to $6.4B (high), with a median of $3.6B. This range reflects material uncertainty around Modal's positioning within a heterogeneous peer set. The key uncertainties are the appropriate weighting between stage-progression and peer-relative methods, and whether Modal's characteristics align more closely with infrastructure plays like WEKA ($1.6B) or frontier models like Crusoe ($10.0B).
